name: accordion
description: A vertically stacked list of disclosure panels (PARTIAL tier, controlled/uncontrolled). accordion.jte is a styled container that wraps accordion-item.jte instances. Each item is a native <details>/<summary> pair: zero JS, zero server round-trip just to open. Uncontrolled = browser manages open/close state. Controlled = caller binds open="${serverFact}" on each item (the server reflects which panels are expanded; the item is a pure presentation shell). Single-open grouping: all sibling items share the same name= attribute; the platform <details name=...> group enforces at most one open at a time. Variants: default (bordered card, --lv-shadow-xs), ghost (dividers only, transparent outer), borderless (fully flat). No enhancer: keyboard + aria-expanded + Tab order are all platform-supplied (native <details>/<summary>). APG Accordion pattern. Requires accordion.css for chevron rotation.
docs: Copy accordion.jte + accordion/item.jte + accordion.css to your lievit/ JTE root. Use @template.lievit.accordion(variant=..., content=@`...`) as the container and @template.lievit.accordion-item(label=..., id=..., content=@`...`) for each panel. Uncontrolled: omit open= on items (browser persists state). Controlled: bind open="${serverField.contains(itemId)}" on each item (the server renders truth; no @Wire on the accordion itself -- the CALLER's WIRE component owns the state). Single-open mode: give all sibling items the same name= attribute -- the browser enforces at most one open. Disabled items: disabled=true on the item (aria-disabled + pointer-events-none on the summary). Suppress the panel landmark role via panelRole="" when content is trivial. Link accordion.css (or paste its rules) for chevron rotation; the chevron transition reads --lv-duration-fast + --lv-ease from your token sheet.
